title: musical-organogram-represents
prompt: Create the Biomorphic Cello: living strings that evolve new harmonic possibilities...
answer: This organogram represents a simple musical instrument with an idiophone (circle) and an aerophone (rectangle). The idiophone symbolizes the resonant body, while the aerophone indicates the air column or reed mechanism.
summary: This organogram represents a simple musical instrument with an idiophone (circle) and an aerophone (rectangle). The idiophone symbolizes the resonant body, while the aerophone indicates the air column or reed mechanism.
materials_text:
1 High-density Spruce soundboard
2 Carbon fiber reinforced polymers for structural frames
code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
plt.style.use('dark_background')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.set_xlim(-2, 2)
ax.set_ylim(-2, 2)
ax.axis('off')

# Idiophone (circle) - resonant body
idiophone = plt.Circle((0.5, 0), radius=0.4, color='orange', fill=True)
ax.add_patch(idiophone)

# Aerophone (rectangle) - air column or reed mechanism
aerophone = plt.Rectangle((-0.8, -1), width=2, height=0.6, color='grey', fill=True)
ax.add_patch(aerophone)

plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
plt.style.use('dark_background')

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))
ax.set_xlim(-2, 2)
ax.set_ylim(-2, 2)
ax.axis('off')

# Idiophone (circle) - resonant body
idiophone = plt.Circle((0.5, 0), radius=0.4, color='orange', fill=True)
ax.add_patch(idiophone)

# Aerophone (rectangle) - air column or reed mechanism
aerophone = plt.Rectangle((-0.8, -1), width=2, height=0.6, color='grey', fill=True)
ax.add_patch(aerophone)

plt.show()
stl_code:
import numpy as np
import trimesh

# map: air-column -> cylinder, bell radiation -> cone, mouthpiece interface -> cylinder
body = trimesh.creation.cylinder(radius=8.36, height=226.2, sections=59)
bell = trimesh.creation.cone(radius=12.46, height=59.95, sections=59)
bell.apply_translation((0, 0, -143.07))
mouth = trimesh.creation.cylinder(radius=4.36, height=37.4, sections=59)
mouth.apply_translation((0, 0, 131.8))
spine = trimesh.creation.box(extents=(6.69, 9.2, 90.48))
mesh = trimesh.util.concatenate([body, bell, mouth, spine])
